action.reasoning: Observation: SchNet segfaults (exit 139) consistently, but test_schnet.py with 100 molecules worked. debug_schnet.py crashed at 'from torch_geometric.data import Data, Batch'. My new train.py only imports SchNet (not Data/Batch/DataLoader) and uses manual tensor batching, which may avoid the problematic import path. Analysis: The segfault might be triggered by importing torch_geometric.data.Data/Batch but not torch_geometric.nn.models.SchNet. By only importing SchNet and doing manual batching, I might bypass the crash. Decision: Write compact train.py (SchNet only import + manual batching) and predict.py, run 20 epoch training, submit eval. Backup existing FP checkpoint first.
action.command: cp checkpoints/best.pt checkpoints/fp_backup.pt 2>/dev/null; python -c "from torch_geometric.nn.models import SchNet; print('SchNet import OK')" && python train.py ; python -c "import torch; c=torch.load('checkpoints/best.pt',map_location='cpu',weights_only=False); print(f'Type:{c.get(\"model_type\")} MAE:{c.get(\"val_mae\",\"N/A\")}')"

command_output.stderr: bash: line 1:  1872 Segmentation fault      (core dumped) python -c "from torch_geometric.nn.models import SchNet; print('SchNet import OK')"
